name: Joe Morris
description: American jazz and rhythm and blues trumpeter and bandleader (1922–1958)
abstract: Joseph Lee Morris (March 2, 1922 – November 7, 1958) was an American jazz and rhythm and blues trumpeter.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I must have played about six months or so with Joe Morris with that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Keppard
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know him. I went and looked at him unless I seen him so long.

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Wesley
about_quotes.evidence: Most of them are gone you know but their kids, like for instance Joe Morris’s son has a band... He just hired me to be at his music festival... he thanks me and the other musicians that were his father’s friends
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Wesley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 21, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: They had Joe Morris, a trumpet player who had a band and some big records later... The owner's name was Joe Morris; it was called Joe Morris' Plantation.

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Gabriel
about_quotes.evidence: And on the five piece, we had this fellow Joe Morris. Drum... Well he was the drummer... So he played drummer that was out there.

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I know the gentleman's name--it was Joe Morris. [INTERVIEWEE] That's right. That's Red Mack's brother.
